theory.intro: Word problems with money, lengths and masses often need decimals to be added or subtracted. Line up the decimal points and work carefully.
facts: **Line up the decimal points** when adding or subtracting decimals.
Fill missing decimal places with zeros so the numbers have the same length.
steps: Read the problem and decide whether to add or subtract.
Write the decimals with their points aligned.
Add or subtract and check the answer is reasonable.
